items.title: AMD releases FSR 4.1 for RX 9000-series GPUs — new update delivers better Ray Regeneration, finer upscaled detail, and higher FPS
items.description: AMD has added Ray Regeneration 1.1 and FSR 4.1 upscaling to its RDNA 4 GPUs, bringing it to parity with Sony's PSSR 2 on the PS5 Pro. Games that support these features will have better ray tracing quality with more accurate shadow detail, while also getting a sharper-looking image through ML-based upscaling.

Subscribe to our newsletter AMD has just released a new graphics driver that enables the latest edition of its upscaling tech for RDNA 4 GPUs: FSR 4.1. This is an iterative update, but it does bring official support for Crimson Desert (and Death Stranding 2), adding Ray Regeneration 1.1 to the game, along with improving the base upscaler for "ML-powered" FSR games. The Ultra Performance Mode is now faster, too, delivering higher FPS than before.🚀 Crimson Desert is Live Today!Continuing our journey on @AMD FSR Redstone and announcing our new GPU driver release is ready:✨ Ray Regeneration 1.1 – Enhanced reflections & global illumination, fully supported in Crimson Desert🎮 FSR Upscaling 4.1 – Sharper image quality… pic.twitter.com/rvSu4kA1aLMarch 19, 2026Most notably, FSR 4.1 improves the quality of AMD's AI-enhanced upscaling. Games that could previously be upgraded to FSR 4 via driver override will now use the upgraded 4.1 model for this purpose. The new model appears to offer more detail in objects in motion, especially foliage. AMD showed an example where grass looked significantly sharper and less blocked-up like with FSR 4.0. Finer details like these usually become subject to artifacting or just look blurry with less advanced upscaling approaches, but FSR 4.1 seems to be handling them well, and better than the first FSR 4 model. (Image credit: AMD)The latest driver also upgrades FSR Ray Regeneration to version 1.1. Ray Regeneration is AMD’s way of denoising a limited number of ray-traced samples and rebuilding them into a higher-quality final image, similar to Nvidia's DLSS Ray Reconstruction. AMD showed Ray Regeneration 1.1 producing better contrast in scenes, with much more realistic shadows, alongside the potential benefits of RT for global illumination and enhanced reflections.Article continues below You may like Optiscaler team fixes INT8 FSR 4 ghosting on RX 6000 series GPUs AMD hints at officially open-sourcing FSR 4 Nvidia introduces DLSS 4.5 and Multi Frame Generation 6X at CES 2026 Image credit: AMD Image credit: AMD Lastly, AMD also talked up Ultra Performance Mode, where the game is rendered at a low internal resolution to maintain high FPS. Apparently, it's also getting a performance boost, squeezing out even more frames without sacrificing image quality (at least compared to the limitations for IQ already present in Ultra Performance mode). AMD didn't offer specific performance claims for this improvement, though.All of these features were demoed using Crimson Desert, and AMD says at least FSR 4.1 upscaling is available on all "ML-powered" games. That likely means titles that already supported FSR Redstone, but gamers have been able to manually override FSR versions in AMD's Radeon Software since FSR 4 first arrived, so it's likely that replacement will now use FSR 4.1 with the latest driver.As an interesting note, FSR 4.1 uses the same underlying AI model as PlayStation Spectral Super Resolution (PSSR) 2. The second iteration of that tech has just come out for the PS5 Pro, and PlayStation lead system architect Mark Cerny confirmed on X that the two technologies share a common underpinning. PSSR 2 is based on FSR 4.1 and is part of Project Amethyst, Sony and AMD's joint effort to advance AI tech in gaming.Just tried FSR Upscaling 4.1 in a few PC games. It’s based on the same neural network as the upgraded PSSR we released for PS5 Pro… and it looks stunning!Wonderful working with @jackhuynh and the @AMD team as we collaborate on AI graphics tech. Big win for Project Amethyst :-) pic.twitter.com/yhgwJG716EMarch 19, 2026For all its improvements, FSR 4.1 remains limited to RX 9000-series GPUs only. In contrast, Nvidia's DLSS 4.5 upscaler works on much older GeForce GPUs. Unofficial community support for FSR 4 on older Radeons thanks to leaked source code appears not to have moved AMD's official position on the availability of FSR 4 yet, so owners of those older cards will continue to miss out.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
items.author: Hassam Nasir
items.titleZh: AMD发布FSR 4.1技术 适配RX 9000系列显卡——新版本带来更强光线重建、更精细放大细节与更高帧率
items.descriptionZh:
AMD近日发布了新版显卡驱动，为其下一代RDNA 4架构GPU（RX 9000系列）启用了最新版本的超分辨率技术——FSR 4.1。这是一次迭代更新，但带来了多项实质性改进，并为首日发布的游戏《赤色沙漠》（以及即将推出的《死亡搁浅2》）提供了官方支持，为其引入了光线重建1.1技术，同时优化了所有基于“ML驱动”的FSR游戏的基础超分画质。此外，超高性能模式也获得了速度提升。

**核心技术原理与创新点**

此次更新的核心是FSR 4.1超采样技术。它在FSR 4.0的基础上，显著提升了AI增强超分的画质。对于之前能通过驱动程序强制升级到FSR 4的游戏，现在将自动使用升级后的4.1模型。新模型主要改进了动态物体的细节渲染，特别是复杂的植被（如草地）。AMD展示的对比显示，FSR 4.1下的草叶看起来比4.0版本锐利得多，像素块状感大幅减少。这些细微细节在落后的超分技术中通常会出现伪影或模糊，但FSR 4.1处理得更为出色。

另一项重要升级是**光线重建技术更新至1.1版本**。该技术类似于英伟达的DLSS光线重建，通过对有限数量的光线追踪样本进行去噪和重建，生成更高质量的最终图像。Ray Regeneration 1.1提升了场景的对比度，生成更为真实的阴影，并在全局光照和增强反射等光线追踪效果上带来了潜在画质收益。

最后，**超高性能模式**在保持极低内部渲染分辨率以换取高帧率的同时，也获得了性能增强，能够在现有画质水平下挤出更多帧数，不过AMD未提供具体的性能提升数据。

**技术影响与合作关系**

一个值得注意的关联是，AMD确认FSR 4.1与索尼PlayStation Spectral Super Resolution 2.0技术基于相同的底层AI模型。PSSR 2.0是近期为PS5 Pro推出的技术，索尼首席系统架构师马克·塞尼证实了二者的同源性。这源于双方合作的“紫水晶计划”，旨在共同推进游戏AI图形技术。这意味着PC与主机平台在超分技术上实现了底层架构的共享与协同进化。

**应用场景与硬件限制**

所有新功能均在《赤色沙漠》中进行了演示。AMD表示，FSR 4.1超分技术将适用于所有“ML驱动”的FSR游戏，这很可能指原本就支持FSR Redstone技术栈的游戏。同时，玩家仍可通过Radeon Software驱动程序手动覆盖游戏内的FSR版本，并在更新驱动后默认使用FSR 4.1。

然而，**FSR 4.1及其相关技术目前仍仅限于RX 9000系列GPU**。相比之下，英伟达的DLSS 4.5支持更早的GeForce显卡。尽管社区已通过泄露的源代码为旧款Radeon显卡提供了非官方FSR 4支持，但AMD官方尚未改变其硬件支持策略，旧款A卡用户暂时无法享受此次升级。

items.title: Planned 10-gigawatt Softbank data center in Ohio might be the largest in the world — will require a $33 billion natural gas plant, equivalent to nine nuclear reactors
items.description: SoftBank is looking forward to building a $60 billion - $70 billion AI data center spanning multiple phases in Ohio.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter SoftBank Group is prepping to build a new AI data center in Ohio that could reach 10 GW of power demand and cost $30 billion to $40 billion for the computing infrastructure and a further $33 billion for a natural gas power plant, reports Bloomberg. When completed, the new site could be one of the largest AI data centers ever built. Furthermore, it will be powered by one of the world’s largest fleets of gas turbines, equivalent to the energy supply of nine nuclear reactors.SoftBank yet has to disclose which hardware will power the data center, though it looks like this one will feature multiple generations of AI hardware, as the first gas turbines will be installed within a year, meaning that the first phase of the data center will run Nvidia’s Rubin and/or AMD’s Instinct MI455X-class accelerators. Meanwhile, the full deployment is expected by the end of the decade, which means that the facility will also use Nvidia’s Feynman or post-Feynman hardware and/or AMD’s MI600 or even MI700-series, though we are speculating.The proposed data center will be located in a 3,700-acre complex in Piketon, which previously produced weapons-grade uranium during the Cold War and later supported civilian nuclear fuel production before shutting down in 2001. To power the data center, SoftBank-backed SB Energy is deploying a fleet of gas turbines capable of generating approximately 9.2 GW, with an additional 800 MW of capacity also planned, according to the report. These turbines will not be concentrated at a single site, but distributed across the region. The initial turbines will be installed within a year, and full deployment is expected by the end of the decade. In parallel, American Electric Power’s local utility unit will provide transmission and grid infrastructure upgrades worth $4.2 billion, the report claims.Article continues below You may like Stargate AI data centers for OpenAI reportedly delayed by squabbles between partners Meta inks deals to supply a staggering 6 gigawatts in nuclear power for data center ambitions Data center developers building private natural gas 'Shadow Grid' power plants to sidestep strained grids It is noteworthy that the 10 GW requirement represents a substantial share of Ohio’s total generation capacity, which totaled around 30 GW in 2024.Interestingly, the project worth over $66 billion does not seem to be a part of the well-known Stargate project (co-backed by CityBank), but the initiative is a part of a $550 billion U.S.–Japan investment program recently established under a bilateral agreement that also included reductions in U.S. auto tariffs and other duties. As an initial step, the two countries last month unveiled three projects in the U.S. that include an oil export terminal, a natural gas-fired power plant, and a synthetic diamond production facility. The total value of these projects is $36 billion. items.author: Anton Shilov
items.titleZh: 软银拟建10千兆瓦俄亥俄数据中心或成全球之最——需配套330亿美元天然气电厂，相当于九座核反应堆发电量
items.descriptionZh:
软银集团正计划在美国俄亥俄州建设一座全新的人工智能数据中心。根据彭博社报道，该数据中心电力需求预计高达10吉瓦，其计算基础设施的建设成本估计在300亿至400亿美元之间，此外还将额外投入330亿美元配套建设一座天然气发电厂。建成后，该设施有望成为全球规模最大的AI数据中心之一，并由世界上最大规模的燃气轮机阵列之一提供动力，其发电能力相当于九座核反应堆。

项目选址位于派克顿一处占地3700英亩的园区，该地曾在冷战时期生产武器级铀，并在2001年关停前支持民用核燃料生产。为了满足数据中心的巨大能源需求，由软银支持的SB Energy公司将部署一批总发电能力约为9.2吉瓦的燃气轮机，并计划额外增加800兆瓦的发电容量。这些涡轮机不会集中在一个地点，而是将分布在整个区域。首批涡轮机预计在一年内安装，全部部署计划在本十年末完成。此外，根据报告，美国电力公司的当地公用事业部门将投资42亿美元用于升级输电和电网基础设施。

值得关注的是，10吉瓦的电力需求占据了俄亥俄州2024年总发电容量（约30吉瓦）的相当大一部分，凸显了项目对区域能源供应的巨大影响。

在技术路线方面，虽然软银尚未正式公布将采用何种硬件，但可以推断该数据中心将采用多代AI硬件。由于首批涡轮机将在一年内就位，这意味着数据中心的第一阶段很可能会搭载英伟达的Rubin平台和/或AMD的Instinct MI455X级加速器。而整个项目的全面部署预计要到本十年末完成，届时该设施很可能也将使用英伟达的Feynman或其后续平台，和/或AMD的MI600甚至MI700系列加速器。

有趣的是，这个总投资超过660亿美元的庞大项目，似乎并非此前广为人知的由CityBank等共同支持的“星际之门”项目的一部分。相反，它是近期根据一项双边协议建立的日美5500亿美元投资计划中的一环，该协议还包括降低美国汽车关税等条款。作为该计划的第一步，两国上个月已公布了在美国的三个项目，包括一个石油出口终端、一座天然气发电厂和一个合成钻石生产设施，总投资价值360亿美元。

这一项目不仅是AI基础设施领域的巨大投资，也反映了超大规模数据中心对集中式、高容量专用能源的迫切需求，以及为应对电网压力而采取的“影子电网”建设趋势。其技术硬件的跨代布局，也预示了未来几年AI算力竞赛的持续升级。

items.title: Why people really hate AI
items.description:
There's a big, and increasing, disconnect in culture right now when it comes to artificial intelligence. Companies of all shapes and sizes are hunting for places to deploy AI and can't stop talking about how this new technology will change everything. But when you ask people about AI, the consistent response is: no thanks. Study after study shows people are worried about the effects of AI and simply don't find the technology worth all the downsides.

items.content: There’s a big, and increasing, disconnect in culture right now when it comes to artificial intelligence. Companies of all shapes and sizes are hunting for places to deploy AI and can’t stop talking about how this new technology will change everything. But when you ask people about AI, the consistent response is: no thanks. Study after study shows people are worried about the effects of AI and simply don’t find the technology worth all the downsides.Verge subscribers, don’t forget you get exclusive access to ad-free Vergecast wherever you get your podcasts. Head here. Not a subscriber? You can sign up here.On this episode of The Vergecast, David and Nilay try to understand the AI gap. No matter what you’ve heard, it’s not the water worries that are keeping AI from succeeding. Nor is it the supposedly “doomer” CEOs. The problem, we think, is simpler than all that. AI may be interesting, valuable business software, and it may make writing code easier than ever. But we are still without a truly game-changing consumer use case for AI — at least, one people are willing to pay for.After that, The Verge’s Allison Johnson joins the show with a suddenly in-demand device, the Samsung Galaxy Z TriFold. Allison has spent months trying to get one of these phones, only to have Samsung discontinue the device this week. She takes us through her journey with the phone and helps us figure out what’s next for foldables, if anything.Finally, in the lightning round, it’s time for another round of Brendan Carr is a Dummy, DLSS 5, the latest news on the metaverse, and some unfortunate news about a couple of viral stories on social media. If you want to know more about everything we discuss in this episode, here are some links to get you started, first on AI:OpenAI cuts back on “side quests.”Signalsandnoise on TikTokFrom NBC News: Poll: Majority of voters say risks of AI outweigh the benefitsFrom Pew: How Americans View AI and Its Impact on Human Abilities, SocietyAnd in foldable phones:PSA: Don’t buy a $4,400 gray market Samsung TriFold on eBaySamsung discontinues its Galaxy Z TriFold after just three months Oppo’s nearly creaseless foldable isn’t launching in Europe after all From last year: Just look at Huawei’s trifold phoneAnd in the lightning round:Brendan Carr’s tweet threatening broadcasters about war coverageJensen Huang, on the critical reaction to DLSS 5: “Well, first of all, they’re completely wrong.”DLSS 5 looks like a real-time generative AI filter for video games Nvidia has lost the plot with gamersMeta is actually keeping its VR metaverse running, for nowThis is not a fly uploaded to a computerChatGPT did not cure a dog’s cancer
items.author: David Pierce
items.titleZh: 为何人们真正讨厌人工智能
items.descriptionZh:
当前人工智能领域正面临显著且日益扩大的认知鸿沟。尽管各类企业争相部署AI技术并不断渲染其变革性潜力，但多项民调显示，公众对AI的接受度持续低迷——人们普遍担忧其潜在风险，认为这项技术带来的弊端可能超过收益。

在最新一期《The Vergecast》播客中，主持人深入探讨了这一矛盾现象。讨论指出，阻碍AI普及的关键并非算力瓶颈或所谓“末日论”CEO的警告，而是一个更本质的问题：尽管AI在企业软件和代码生成等领域展现价值，但至今仍未出现真正颠覆性的消费级应用场景，尤其是用户愿意为之付费的“杀手级”产品。

节目随后聚焦消费硬件领域。三星Galaxy Z TriFold三折叠手机在引发市场短暂关注后，上市仅三个月便突然停产。《The Verge》科技记者分享了数月来的实测体验，并借此探讨折叠屏设备的未来发展方向——当激进创新遭遇市场冷遇，这类产品究竟是需要更成熟的迭代，还是本身缺乏真实需求？

在快讯环节，本期节目还涉及多个热点事件：美国联邦通信委员会委员布伦丹·卡因争议性言论被批“缺乏常识”；英伟达DLSS 5技术因被指“更像实时AI滤镜”引发游戏圈争议；Meta仍艰难维持其VR元宇宙平台运营；以及两则社交媒体谣言的澄清——所谓“上传苍蝇意识至计算机”的实验实为误读，而ChatGPT治愈宠物癌症的消息亦属虚构。

items.title: Supermicro employees accused of smuggling $2.5 billion worth of Nvidia hardware to China — perps used a hairdryer to move serial numbers between real hardware and thousands of dummy servers
items.description: Three Super Micro employees have been charged with conspiring to unlawfully divert cutting edge U.S. artificial intelligence technology to China.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter Three Super Micro employees have been charged with conspiring to unlawfully divert cutting-edge U.S. artificial intelligence technology to China. An indictment unsealed on Thursday says Yih-Shyan “Wally” Liaw, Ruei-Tsang “Steven” Chang, and Ting-Wei “Willy” Sun were involved in a smuggling plot that leveraged a middleman Southeast Asian company to fake paperwork and repackage servers that were powered by cutting-edge Nvidia chips. The underhanded operation is thought to have netted roughly $2.5b in sales since 2024.Super Micro isn’t named in the indictment, but Liaw is a co-founder of the server firm, with nearly half a billion dollars worth of shares under his belt (the shares just dropped 12% in value on this news). Moreover, Chang is a sales manager for Super Micro in Taiwan. Sun is referred to as a third-party broker and fixer who has worked with the other two at Super Micro previously. Liaw (71, a U.S. citizen), and Sun (44, Taiwan) have been arrested, but Chang (53, Taiwan) is currently a fugitive, notes the Justice Department PR.The transshipment scheme exposedBasically, billions of dollars of Nvidia-powered servers that shouldn’t have been available to Chinese customers were funneled to the country using a fake front company, which fabricated paperwork and assembled thousands of dummy servers, to fool inspectors. It was far from a casual or opportunistic operation, with a high level of coordinated deception.Article continues below You may like Former Chinese gaming company with China govt ties accused of smuggling banned AI GPUs Ten former Samsung employees arrested for industrial espionage charges for giving China chipmaker 10nm tech Former Google engineer convicted of stealing GPU and TPU trade secrets for 'Chinese interests' Despite the sophistication of the covert documentation, logistics, and supporting operations, some analysts have highlighted the brazen absurdity of the operation. For example, the indicted trio have been accused of maintaining a substantial inventory of thousands of dummy (empty shell) servers in Southeast Asia, supposedly awaiting deployment to local (not China-based) customers.I'm sorry but this super micro thing is awful but parts of it are genuinely hilarious They literally used a hair dryer to move serial numbers from real servers to dummy servers to throw in a warehouse and got caught on camera pic.twitter.com/Ht9gBBF7aQMarch 20, 2026Analyst Max Weinbach highlights some CCTV image captures showing workers using hair dryers to transfer serial number stickers from genuine servers to the dummy server shells. They would subsequently ship the real GPU-packed servers to China.Today, due to the ever-shifting sands of U.S. export controls policy, some of the smuggled Super Micro servers might be approved as exportable after following a framework to get a license.Super Micro and Nvidia aren’t in the FBI’s crosshairsSuper Micro has acknowledged that the accused trio are “associated” with it, but highlights that the company isn’t named as a defendant in the indictment. The official statement says that it has “placed the two employees on administrative leave and terminated its relationship with the contractor, effective immediately.”Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsAt the same time, the server maker distanced itself from the actions of the trio. The smuggling caper was described as “a contravention of the Company's policies,” and Super Micro “maintains a robust compliance program and is committed to full adherence to all applicable U.S. export and re-export control laws and regulations.”Nvidia sent a similar themed statement to Tom’s Hardware. “Strict compliance is a top priority for Nvidia. We continue to work closely with our customers and the government on compliance programs as export regulations have expanded,” wrote the AI and graphics chip maker. “Unlawful diversion of controlled U.S. computers to China is a losing proposition across the board—Nvidia does not provide any service or support for such systems, and the enforcement mechanisms are rigorous and effective.”Liaw, Chang, and Sun face some very serious federal felony charges. It is early days in the investigation, but it is possible that these people, in trying to enrich themselves, will end up serving decades (up to 30 years) behind bars, and/or fines, asset forfeiture, and also bans from employment in export-controlled industries. items.titleZh: 超微员工被指控向中国走私价值25亿美元英伟达硬件——案犯使用吹风机在真硬件与数千台假服务器间转移序列号
items.descriptionZh:
近日，美国司法部公布的一份起诉书揭露了一起涉及向中国非法转移尖端人工智能技术的重大案件，三名与服务器制造商超微（Super Micro）有关联的人员被指控参与了一项复杂的走私计划。

**背景与涉案人员**
起诉书指出，美国公民廖逸贤（Yih-Shian "Wally" Liaw，71岁）、台湾地区居民张瑞苍（Ruei-Tsang "Steven" Chang，53岁）与孙廷玮（Ting-Wei "Willy" Sun，44岁）涉嫌合谋，将搭载先进英伟达芯片的服务器非法运往中国。廖逸贤是超微公司的联合创始人，持有价值近5亿美元的公司股份；张瑞苍则是超微在台湾地区的销售经理；孙廷玮则是一名曾与两人合作的第三方经纪人和协调人。目前廖逸贤和孙廷玮已被逮捕，张瑞苍在逃。受此消息影响，超微股价一度下跌12%。

**核心技术原理与走私手法**
该计划的核心是利用美国出口管制法规的漏洞，将本不应售予中国客户的高性能计算设备（即搭载高端英伟达AI GPU的服务器）非法转运。其技术原理并非芯片本身的创新，而是构建了一套精密的物流与文件欺诈体系：
1.  **设立空壳公司**：在东南亚利用一家中间公司作为幌子，伪造最终用户信息。
2.  **伪造文书与“假服务器”**：伪造大量出口文件，并制造了数千台“假服务器”（仅有机箱外壳的空壳设备），用以应付监管检查，营造设备将运往非中国客户的假象。
3.  **“调包”操作**：起诉书和曝光的监控画面显示，涉案人员甚至在仓库中使用吹风机等工具，将真服务器上的序列号标签转移到假服务器外壳上。随后，将拆出的、装载有实际GPU芯片的真服务器部件秘密运往中国。
自2024年以来，该走私网络据信已实现了约25亿美元的销售额。分析人士指出，尽管操作手法周密，但其维持大量假服务器库存、使用吹风机转移标签等行为也显得颇为“荒谬”和大胆。

**性能参数与市场影响**
走私所涉及的是受美国出口管制的高端英伟达AI加速器（如H100、A100等系列）。这些芯片是训练大型语言模型（LLM）和进行高性能计算（HPC）的关键硬件，算力远超对华出口的合规替代品。该案件凸显了国际市场，特别是中国AI行业，对顶级算力的强劲需求与美国出口管制之间的激烈矛盾。值得注意的是，随着美国出口管制政策的动态调整，部分被走私的服务器型号现在可能已能通过申请许可证的方式合法出口。

**公司回应与技术合规影响**
超微公司和英伟达均未在本次起诉中被列为被告。超微发布声明，确认两名涉案员工已被停职，与第三方承包商的关系已被立即终止。公司强调此次事件“违反了公司的政策”，并表示自身拥有健全的合规计划，致力于完全遵守所有美国出口法规。
英伟达也向媒体表示，严格合规是其首要任务，公司持续与客户和政府合作推进合规项目，并指出非法向中国转移受控计算机系统是“全盘皆输的命题”，英伟达不会为此类系统提供任何服务或支持，且执法机制是严格有效的。

**结论与潜在后果**
此案件远非偶然的投机行为，而是一项高度协调、长期运作的走私计划。它严重挑战了美国对华技术出口管制的执行效力，也反映出在全球AI竞赛背景下，尖端算力已成为高度敏感的战略物资。三名被告面临多项联邦重罪指控，若罪名成立，可能面临最高30年的监禁、巨额罚款、资产没收以及被禁止从事受出口管制行业工作等严厉惩罚。这一事件预计将促使监管机构和企业进一步加强供应链审查与合规措施。

items.title: Arctic's $1,400 AMD Strix Point fanless mini-PC hides under your desk — Senza AI 370 features Ryzen AI 9 HX 370 CPU, 32GB RAM, and 1TB SSD
items.description: If you've been looking for a mini PC that goes beyond just decluttering your desk — one that basically doesn't even remind you of its existence, then Arctic has got just the thing for you. The new Senza AI 370 features a powerful AMD chip with a decent iGPU, 32 GB of fast RAM, a 1 TB SSD, and plenty of ports. It costs almost $1,400, though, but at least it's fanless.

Subscribe to our newsletter Arctic has just released a new mini-PC aimed at enthusiasts looking for a clean, minimalist, and silent setup, without compromising on performance. The "Senza AI 370" is the latest in the company's existing Senza lineup that has shipped with the Ryzen 5000 series so far, but it's now been upgraded to AMD's Strix Point. Specifically, it features the Ryzen AI 9 HX 370 flagship APU and a neat trick up its sleeve.Or should we say "below its sleeve"? Because this mini-PC mounts under your desk, completely out of sight, cable management isn't an issue either. Arctic calls it an "Under Desk PC," and it's designed to clear your workspace. You could put it on top of your desk, but that would defeat its entire purpose. You really want to make sure you get the most out of it because it costs 1200 Euros, or almost $1,400.Image 1 of 3(Image credit: Arctic)(Image credit: Arctic)(Image credit: Arctic)For that money, you're getting a top-end chip, the Ryzen AI 370, which has 12 cores and 24 threads that can boost up to 5.1 GHz. It launched almost two years ago, but it was the Red Team's premier laptop offering until Gorgon Point did so a few months ago. It's based on AMD's Zen 5 architecture, featuring 4 standard Zen 5 cores and 8 Zen 5c efficiency cores — both are split off into their own CCX.Article continues below You may like Akasa launches its latest-generation fanless Mini-ITX case Minisforum MS-02 Ultra mini workstation hands-on GMKtec Evo X2 AI Mini PC review: Compact Strix Halo Powerhouse Testing has shown that this hybrid core config doesn't suit either performance or efficiency, since any tasks requiring more cores spill over to the Zen 5c CCX, and there's a large latency penalty for cross-CCX communication. But that's mostly a concern for mobile devices (since this is a mobile chip), a wall-powered mini-PC circumvents these issues and gives you solid performance regardless.(Image credit: Arctic)The Senza AI 370 looks like an AIO radiator for the most part, down to the two tubes poking out of it that terminate in a front panel hub. This detachable box mounts near the edge of your desk, where you can easily interact with it, as it includes a power button, a couple of USB ports, and a headphone jack. The actual PC is meant to go farther back, toward the rear of your table, where it almost disappears.The port selection on the PC itself is very strong: you get 2.5 Gbps Ethernet, USB4, HDMI 2.1, and more, plus Wi-Fi 7 for wireless connectivity. Owing to its design, the entire body is essentially a giant heatsink, with the actual PCB only in the middle, occupying about 30% of the chassis. Eight heatpipes run through this heatsink, cooling the APU without any fans, which allows it to be completely silent. (Image credit: Arctic)Despite nonexistent noise levels, Arctic is claiming great thermals — 50 degrees Celsius under a gaming load, while a "competitor mini PC" reached 67 degrees Celsius. The company also says the Senza reaches 98 FPS in Counter-Strike 2 (at 1080p High), while a similarly equipped mini PC only reaches 86 FPS. This is possible due to the higher-spec RAM configuration on the Senza.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsIt features 32 GB of LPDDR5X-8000 memory, while many other devices use standard DDR5 at slower speeds. The Ryzen AI 370 also has a Radeon 890M iGPU, which is fairly performant for integrated graphics. It's no Strix Halo, but it will get the job done in lightweight games, and can produce decent results in AAA titles with upscaling. It's got 16 RDNA 3.5 Compute Units and can boost up to 2.9 GHz. (Image credit: Arctic)To round out the device, other specs include a 1 TB PCIe 4.0 SSD, support for up to 8K external monitors, pre-installed Windows 11, and, of course, Microsoft Copilot+ branding for AI workloads. You can buy the Senza AI 370 right away from Arctic's website, and while it does cost 1,200 Euros, we noticed that a 170 Euros payback is automatically applied, bringing the price down to 1,029 Euros ($1,192). That's still quite a lot for this performance tier. Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
items.author: Hassam Nasir
items.titleZh: 雅浚1400美元无风扇迷你主机藏身桌底——Senza AI 370搭载锐龙AI 9 HX 370处理器，配备32GB内存与1TB固态硬盘
items.descriptionZh:
知名散热与PC配件制造商Arctic近日推出了一款面向发烧友的全新迷你PC——Senza AI 370。该产品旨在为用户提供一个简洁、极简且完全静音的高性能计算方案，其核心设计理念是“隐形”，可悬挂于桌面下方，彻底摆脱线缆杂乱并释放桌面空间。作为Senza系列的最新成员，它从原有的Ryzen 5000系列平台升级至AMD最新的Strix Point移动平台，搭载了旗舰级APU Ryzen AI 9 HX 370。

**核心硬件与架构创新**
Senza AI 370的核心是Ryzen AI 9 HX 370 APU。这款芯片采用AMD Zen 5架构，拥有12个核心和24个线程，最高加速频率可达5.1 GHz。其核心配置采用混合设计，包含4个标准性能的Zen 5核心和8个专注于能效的Zen 5c核心，且这两组核心分别位于独立的CCX（核心复合体）中。在移动设备上，这种设计可能因跨CCX通信延迟而影响部分多核任务效率，但作为固定接电的迷你PC，电源不受限，可以充分发挥其持续高性能输出的潜力。

图形部分则由Radeon 890M集成显卡负责，其基于RDNA 3.5架构，拥有16个计算单元，加速频率高达2.9 GHz，足以应对轻量级游戏，并在借助超分辨率技术的3A大作中提供尚可的体验。

**独特的“隐形”设计与静音散热**
该产品的最大亮点是其“桌面下PC”的设计。主机本体可安装在桌面后方或下方，几乎完全隐藏。唯一可见的部分是一个可拆卸的前置面板集线器，通过两根导管与主机连接，其上集成了电源键、USB接口和耳机插孔，方便用户日常操作。这种设计将线缆管理和设备藏匿完美结合。

为了实现绝对的静音，Arctic采用了彻底的无风扇散热方案。整个机身实质上是一个巨大的散热器，内部PCB板仅占机箱约30%的空间。八根热管贯穿于这个大型散热体中，将APU产生的热量高效导出并依靠整个金属机壳被动散热。据Arctic称，在游戏负载下，其温度可控制在50摄氏度，而对比的竞品迷你PC则达到了67摄氏度。

**高性能配置与实测表现**
除了强大的APU，该机还配备了高速的32GB LPDDR5X-8000内存。相比许多使用低速标准DDR5内存的设备，这种高带宽内存显著提升了集成显卡的性能和系统响应速度。Arctic提供的测试数据显示，在《反恐精英2》1080p高画质设置下，Senza AI 370能达到98 FPS，而配置相似的竞品仅为86 FPS。

其他规格包括1TB PCIe 4.0 SSD、2.5Gbps有线网口、USB4、HDMI 2.1接口以及Wi-Fi 7无线连接，支持最高8K的外接显示器。设备预装Windows 11，并带有微软Copilot+标识，表明其具备较强的本地AI运算能力。

**市场定位与价格**
Senza AI 370现已通过Arctic官网销售，官方标价为1200欧元（约合1400美元），但结账时会自动减免170欧元，实际入手价格为1029欧元（约1192美元）。尽管价格处于高端区间，但考虑到其顶级的移动APU、独特的隐形设计、完全的静音体验以及出色的散热性能，它为追求极致简洁桌面美学和安静环境的高端用户与发烧友提供了一个颇具吸引力的集成化解决方案。

history.title: AMD releases FSR 4.1 for RX 9000-series GPUs — new update delivers better Ray Regeneration, finer upscaled detail, and higher FPS
history.description: AMD has added Ray Regeneration 1.1 and FSR 4.1 upscaling to its RDNA 4 GPUs, bringing it to parity with Sony's PSSR 2 on the PS5 Pro. Games that support these features will have better ray tracing quality with more accurate shadow detail, while also getting a sharper-looking image through ML-based upscaling.

Subscribe to our newsletter AMD has just released a new graphics driver that enables the latest edition of its upscaling tech for RDNA 4 GPUs: FSR 4.1. This is an iterative update, but it does bring official support for Crimson Desert (and Death Stranding 2), adding Ray Regeneration 1.1 to the game, along with improving the base upscaler for "ML-powered" FSR games. The Ultra Performance Mode is now faster, too, delivering higher FPS than before.🚀 Crimson Desert is Live Today!Continuing our journey on @AMD FSR Redstone and announcing our new GPU driver release is ready:✨ Ray Regeneration 1.1 – Enhanced reflections & global illumination, fully supported in Crimson Desert🎮 FSR Upscaling 4.1 – Sharper image quality… pic.twitter.com/rvSu4kA1aLMarch 19, 2026Most notably, FSR 4.1 improves the quality of AMD's AI-enhanced upscaling. Games that could previously be upgraded to FSR 4 via driver override will now use the upgraded 4.1 model for this purpose. The new model appears to offer more detail in objects in motion, especially foliage. AMD showed an example where grass looked significantly sharper and less blocked-up like with FSR 4.0. Finer details like these usually become subject to artifacting or just look blurry with less advanced upscaling approaches, but FSR 4.1 seems to be handling them well, and better than the first FSR 4 model. (Image credit: AMD)The latest driver also upgrades FSR Ray Regeneration to version 1.1. Ray Regeneration is AMD’s way of denoising a limited number of ray-traced samples and rebuilding them into a higher-quality final image, similar to Nvidia's DLSS Ray Reconstruction. AMD showed Ray Regeneration 1.1 producing better contrast in scenes, with much more realistic shadows, alongside the potential benefits of RT for global illumination and enhanced reflections.Article continues below You may like Optiscaler team fixes INT8 FSR 4 ghosting on RX 6000 series GPUs AMD hints at officially open-sourcing FSR 4 Nvidia introduces DLSS 4.5 and Multi Frame Generation 6X at CES 2026 Image credit: AMD Image credit: AMD Lastly, AMD also talked up Ultra Performance Mode, where the game is rendered at a low internal resolution to maintain high FPS. Apparently, it's also getting a performance boost, squeezing out even more frames without sacrificing image quality (at least compared to the limitations for IQ already present in Ultra Performance mode). AMD didn't offer specific performance claims for this improvement, though.All of these features were demoed using Crimson Desert, and AMD says at least FSR 4.1 upscaling is available on all "ML-powered" games. That likely means titles that already supported FSR Redstone, but gamers have been able to manually override FSR versions in AMD's Radeon Software since FSR 4 first arrived, so it's likely that replacement will now use FSR 4.1 with the latest driver.As an interesting note, FSR 4.1 uses the same underlying AI model as PlayStation Spectral Super Resolution (PSSR) 2. The second iteration of that tech has just come out for the PS5 Pro, and PlayStation lead system architect Mark Cerny confirmed on X that the two technologies share a common underpinning. PSSR 2 is based on FSR 4.1 and is part of Project Amethyst, Sony and AMD's joint effort to advance AI tech in gaming.Just tried FSR Upscaling 4.1 in a few PC games. It’s based on the same neural network as the upgraded PSSR we released for PS5 Pro… and it looks stunning!Wonderful working with @jackhuynh and the @AMD team as we collaborate on AI graphics tech. Big win for Project Amethyst :-) pic.twitter.com/yhgwJG716EMarch 19, 2026For all its improvements, FSR 4.1 remains limited to RX 9000-series GPUs only. In contrast, Nvidia's DLSS 4.5 upscaler works on much older GeForce GPUs. Unofficial community support for FSR 4 on older Radeons thanks to leaked source code appears not to have moved AMD's official position on the availability of FSR 4 yet, so owners of those older cards will continue to miss out.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.

history.titleZh: AMD发布FSR 4.1技术 适配RX 9000系列显卡——新版本带来更强光线重建、更精细放大细节与更高帧率
history.descriptionZh:
AMD近日发布了新版显卡驱动，为其下一代RDNA 4架构GPU（RX 9000系列）启用了最新版本的超分辨率技术——FSR 4.1。这是一次迭代更新，但带来了多项实质性改进，并为首日发布的游戏《赤色沙漠》（以及即将推出的《死亡搁浅2》）提供了官方支持，为其引入了光线重建1.1技术，同时优化了所有基于“ML驱动”的FSR游戏的基础超分画质。此外，超高性能模式也获得了速度提升。

**核心技术原理与创新点**

此次更新的核心是FSR 4.1超采样技术。它在FSR 4.0的基础上，显著提升了AI增强超分的画质。对于之前能通过驱动程序强制升级到FSR 4的游戏，现在将自动使用升级后的4.1模型。新模型主要改进了动态物体的细节渲染，特别是复杂的植被（如草地）。AMD展示的对比显示，FSR 4.1下的草叶看起来比4.0版本锐利得多，像素块状感大幅减少。这些细微细节在落后的超分技术中通常会出现伪影或模糊，但FSR 4.1处理得更为出色。

另一项重要升级是**光线重建技术更新至1.1版本**。该技术类似于英伟达的DLSS光线重建，通过对有限数量的光线追踪样本进行去噪和重建，生成更高质量的最终图像。Ray Regeneration 1.1提升了场景的对比度，生成更为真实的阴影，并在全局光照和增强反射等光线追踪效果上带来了潜在画质收益。

最后，**超高性能模式**在保持极低内部渲染分辨率以换取高帧率的同时，也获得了性能增强，能够在现有画质水平下挤出更多帧数，不过AMD未提供具体的性能提升数据。

**技术影响与合作关系**

一个值得注意的关联是，AMD确认FSR 4.1与索尼PlayStation Spectral Super Resolution 2.0技术基于相同的底层AI模型。PSSR 2.0是近期为PS5 Pro推出的技术，索尼首席系统架构师马克·塞尼证实了二者的同源性。这源于双方合作的“紫水晶计划”，旨在共同推进游戏AI图形技术。这意味着PC与主机平台在超分技术上实现了底层架构的共享与协同进化。

**应用场景与硬件限制**

所有新功能均在《赤色沙漠》中进行了演示。AMD表示，FSR 4.1超分技术将适用于所有“ML驱动”的FSR游戏，这很可能指原本就支持FSR Redstone技术栈的游戏。同时，玩家仍可通过Radeon Software驱动程序手动覆盖游戏内的FSR版本，并在更新驱动后默认使用FSR 4.1。

然而，**FSR 4.1及其相关技术目前仍仅限于RX 9000系列GPU**。相比之下，英伟达的DLSS 4.5支持更早的GeForce显卡。尽管社区已通过泄露的源代码为旧款Radeon显卡提供了非官方FSR 4支持，但AMD官方尚未改变其硬件支持策略，旧款A卡用户暂时无法享受此次升级。

history.title: Planned 10-gigawatt Softbank data center in Ohio might be the largest in the world — will require a $33 billion natural gas plant, equivalent to nine nuclear reactors
history.description: SoftBank is looking forward to building a $60 billion - $70 billion AI data center spanning multiple phases in Ohio.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Subscribe to our newsletter SoftBank Group is prepping to build a new AI data center in Ohio that could reach 10 GW of power demand and cost $30 billion to $40 billion for the computing infrastructure and a further $33 billion for a natural gas power plant, reports Bloomberg. When completed, the new site could be one of the largest AI data centers ever built. Furthermore, it will be powered by one of the world’s largest fleets of gas turbines, equivalent to the energy supply of nine nuclear reactors.SoftBank yet has to disclose which hardware will power the data center, though it looks like this one will feature multiple generations of AI hardware, as the first gas turbines will be installed within a year, meaning that the first phase of the data center will run Nvidia’s Rubin and/or AMD’s Instinct MI455X-class accelerators. Meanwhile, the full deployment is expected by the end of the decade, which means that the facility will also use Nvidia’s Feynman or post-Feynman hardware and/or AMD’s MI600 or even MI700-series, though we are speculating.The proposed data center will be located in a 3,700-acre complex in Piketon, which previously produced weapons-grade uranium during the Cold War and later supported civilian nuclear fuel production before shutting down in 2001. To power the data center, SoftBank-backed SB Energy is deploying a fleet of gas turbines capable of generating approximately 9.2 GW, with an additional 800 MW of capacity also planned, according to the report. These turbines will not be concentrated at a single site, but distributed across the region. The initial turbines will be installed within a year, and full deployment is expected by the end of the decade. In parallel, American Electric Power’s local utility unit will provide transmission and grid infrastructure upgrades worth $4.2 billion, the report claims.Article continues below You may like Stargate AI data centers for OpenAI reportedly delayed by squabbles between partners Meta inks deals to supply a staggering 6 gigawatts in nuclear power for data center ambitions Data center developers building private natural gas 'Shadow Grid' power plants to sidestep strained grids It is noteworthy that the 10 GW requirement represents a substantial share of Ohio’s total generation capacity, which totaled around 30 GW in 2024.Interestingly, the project worth over $66 billion does not seem to be a part of the well-known Stargate project (co-backed by CityBank), but the initiative is a part of a $550 billion U.S.–Japan investment program recently established under a bilateral agreement that also included reductions in U.S. auto tariffs and other duties. As an initial step, the two countries last month unveiled three projects in the U.S. that include an oil export terminal, a natural gas-fired power plant, and a synthetic diamond production facility. The total value of these projects is $36 billion. history.author: Anton Shilov
history.titleZh: 软银拟建10千兆瓦俄亥俄数据中心或成全球之最——需配套330亿美元天然气电厂，相当于九座核反应堆发电量
history.descriptionZh:
软银集团正计划在美国俄亥俄州建设一座全新的人工智能数据中心。根据彭博社报道，该数据中心电力需求预计高达10吉瓦，其计算基础设施的建设成本估计在300亿至400亿美元之间，此外还将额外投入330亿美元配套建设一座天然气发电厂。建成后，该设施有望成为全球规模最大的AI数据中心之一，并由世界上最大规模的燃气轮机阵列之一提供动力，其发电能力相当于九座核反应堆。

项目选址位于派克顿一处占地3700英亩的园区，该地曾在冷战时期生产武器级铀，并在2001年关停前支持民用核燃料生产。为了满足数据中心的巨大能源需求，由软银支持的SB Energy公司将部署一批总发电能力约为9.2吉瓦的燃气轮机，并计划额外增加800兆瓦的发电容量。这些涡轮机不会集中在一个地点，而是将分布在整个区域。首批涡轮机预计在一年内安装，全部部署计划在本十年末完成。此外，根据报告，美国电力公司的当地公用事业部门将投资42亿美元用于升级输电和电网基础设施。

值得关注的是，10吉瓦的电力需求占据了俄亥俄州2024年总发电容量（约30吉瓦）的相当大一部分，凸显了项目对区域能源供应的巨大影响。

在技术路线方面，虽然软银尚未正式公布将采用何种硬件，但可以推断该数据中心将采用多代AI硬件。由于首批涡轮机将在一年内就位，这意味着数据中心的第一阶段很可能会搭载英伟达的Rubin平台和/或AMD的Instinct MI455X级加速器。而整个项目的全面部署预计要到本十年末完成，届时该设施很可能也将使用英伟达的Feynman或其后续平台，和/或AMD的MI600甚至MI700系列加速器。

有趣的是，这个总投资超过660亿美元的庞大项目，似乎并非此前广为人知的由CityBank等共同支持的“星际之门”项目的一部分。相反，它是近期根据一项双边协议建立的日美5500亿美元投资计划中的一环，该协议还包括降低美国汽车关税等条款。作为该计划的第一步，两国上个月已公布了在美国的三个项目，包括一个石油出口终端、一座天然气发电厂和一个合成钻石生产设施，总投资价值360亿美元。

这一项目不仅是AI基础设施领域的巨大投资，也反映了超大规模数据中心对集中式、高容量专用能源的迫切需求，以及为应对电网压力而采取的“影子电网”建设趋势。其技术硬件的跨代布局，也预示了未来几年AI算力竞赛的持续升级。

history.title: Why people really hate AI
history.description:
There's a big, and increasing, disconnect in culture right now when it comes to artificial intelligence. Companies of all shapes and sizes are hunting for places to deploy AI and can't stop talking about how this new technology will change everything. But when you ask people about AI, the consistent response is: no thanks. Study after study shows people are worried about the effects of AI and simply don't find the technology worth all the downsides.

On this episode of The Verge …
Read the full story at The Verge.
history.content: There’s a big, and increasing, disconnect in culture right now when it comes to artificial intelligence. Companies of all shapes and sizes are hunting for places to deploy AI and can’t stop talking about how this new technology will change everything. But when you ask people about AI, the consistent response is: no thanks. Study after study shows people are worried about the effects of AI and simply don’t find the technology worth all the downsides.Verge subscribers, don’t forget you get exclusive access to ad-free Vergecast wherever you get your podcasts. Head here. Not a subscriber? You can sign up here.On this episode of The Vergecast, David and Nilay try to understand the AI gap. No matter what you’ve heard, it’s not the water worries that are keeping AI from succeeding. Nor is it the supposedly “doomer” CEOs. The problem, we think, is simpler than all that. AI may be interesting, valuable business software, and it may make writing code easier than ever. But we are still without a truly game-changing consumer use case for AI — at least, one people are willing to pay for.After that, The Verge’s Allison Johnson joins the show with a suddenly in-demand device, the Samsung Galaxy Z TriFold. Allison has spent months trying to get one of these phones, only to have Samsung discontinue the device this week. She takes us through her journey with the phone and helps us figure out what’s next for foldables, if anything.Finally, in the lightning round, it’s time for another round of Brendan Carr is a Dummy, DLSS 5, the latest news on the metaverse, and some unfortunate news about a couple of viral stories on social media. If you want to know more about everything we discuss in this episode, here are some links to get you started, first on AI:OpenAI cuts back on “side quests.”Signalsandnoise on TikTokFrom NBC News: Poll: Majority of voters say risks of AI outweigh the benefitsFrom Pew: How Americans View AI and Its Impact on Human Abilities, SocietyAnd in foldable phones:PSA: Don’t buy a $4,400 gray market Samsung TriFold on eBaySamsung discontinues its Galaxy Z TriFold after just three months Oppo’s nearly creaseless foldable isn’t launching in Europe after all From last year: Just look at Huawei’s trifold phoneAnd in the lightning round:Brendan Carr’s tweet threatening broadcasters about war coverageJensen Huang, on the critical reaction to DLSS 5: “Well, first of all, they’re completely wrong.”DLSS 5 looks like a real-time generative AI filter for video games Nvidia has lost the plot with gamersMeta is actually keeping its VR metaverse running, for nowThis is not a fly uploaded to a computerChatGPT did not cure a dog’s cancer
history.author: David Pierce
history.titleZh: 为何人们真正讨厌人工智能
history.descriptionZh:
当前人工智能领域正面临显著且日益扩大的认知鸿沟。尽管各类企业争相部署AI技术并不断渲染其变革性潜力，但多项民调显示，公众对AI的接受度持续低迷——人们普遍担忧其潜在风险，认为这项技术带来的弊端可能超过收益。

在最新一期《The Vergecast》播客中，主持人深入探讨了这一矛盾现象。讨论指出，阻碍AI普及的关键并非算力瓶颈或所谓“末日论”CEO的警告，而是一个更本质的问题：尽管AI在企业软件和代码生成等领域展现价值，但至今仍未出现真正颠覆性的消费级应用场景，尤其是用户愿意为之付费的“杀手级”产品。

节目随后聚焦消费硬件领域。三星Galaxy Z TriFold三折叠手机在引发市场短暂关注后，上市仅三个月便突然停产。《The Verge》科技记者分享了数月来的实测体验，并借此探讨折叠屏设备的未来发展方向——当激进创新遭遇市场冷遇，这类产品究竟是需要更成熟的迭代，还是本身缺乏真实需求？

在快讯环节，本期节目还涉及多个热点事件：美国联邦通信委员会委员布伦丹·卡因争议性言论被批“缺乏常识”；英伟达DLSS 5技术因被指“更像实时AI滤镜”引发游戏圈争议；Meta仍艰难维持其VR元宇宙平台运营；以及两则社交媒体谣言的澄清——所谓“上传苍蝇意识至计算机”的实验实为误读，而ChatGPT治愈宠物癌症的消息亦属虚构。

history.title: Supermicro employees accused of smuggling $2.5 billion worth of Nvidia hardware to China — perps used a hairdryer to move serial numbers between real hardware and thousands of dummy servers
history.description: Three Super Micro employees have been charged with conspiring to unlawfully divert cutting edge U.S. artificial intelligence technology to China.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Subscribe to our newsletter Three Super Micro employees have been charged with conspiring to unlawfully divert cutting-edge U.S. artificial intelligence technology to China. An indictment unsealed on Thursday says Yih-Shyan “Wally” Liaw, Ruei-Tsang “Steven” Chang, and Ting-Wei “Willy” Sun were involved in a smuggling plot that leveraged a middleman Southeast Asian company to fake paperwork and repackage servers that were powered by cutting-edge Nvidia chips. The underhanded operation is thought to have netted roughly $2.5b in sales since 2024.Super Micro isn’t named in the indictment, but Liaw is a co-founder of the server firm, with nearly half a billion dollars worth of shares under his belt (the shares just dropped 12% in value on this news). Moreover, Chang is a sales manager for Super Micro in Taiwan. Sun is referred to as a third-party broker and fixer who has worked with the other two at Super Micro previously. Liaw (71, a U.S. citizen), and Sun (44, Taiwan) have been arrested, but Chang (53, Taiwan) is currently a fugitive, notes the Justice Department PR.The transshipment scheme exposedBasically, billions of dollars of Nvidia-powered servers that shouldn’t have been available to Chinese customers were funneled to the country using a fake front company, which fabricated paperwork and assembled thousands of dummy servers, to fool inspectors. It was far from a casual or opportunistic operation, with a high level of coordinated deception.Article continues below You may like Former Chinese gaming company with China govt ties accused of smuggling banned AI GPUs Ten former Samsung employees arrested for industrial espionage charges for giving China chipmaker 10nm tech Former Google engineer convicted of stealing GPU and TPU trade secrets for 'Chinese interests' Despite the sophistication of the covert documentation, logistics, and supporting operations, some analysts have highlighted the brazen absurdity of the operation. For example, the indicted trio have been accused of maintaining a substantial inventory of thousands of dummy (empty shell) servers in Southeast Asia, supposedly awaiting deployment to local (not China-based) customers.I'm sorry but this super micro thing is awful but parts of it are genuinely hilarious They literally used a hair dryer to move serial numbers from real servers to dummy servers to throw in a warehouse and got caught on camera pic.twitter.com/Ht9gBBF7aQMarch 20, 2026Analyst Max Weinbach highlights some CCTV image captures showing workers using hair dryers to transfer serial number stickers from genuine servers to the dummy server shells. They would subsequently ship the real GPU-packed servers to China.Today, due to the ever-shifting sands of U.S. export controls policy, some of the smuggled Super Micro servers might be approved as exportable after following a framework to get a license.Super Micro and Nvidia aren’t in the FBI’s crosshairsSuper Micro has acknowledged that the accused trio are “associated” with it, but highlights that the company isn’t named as a defendant in the indictment. The official statement says that it has “placed the two employees on administrative leave and terminated its relationship with the contractor, effective immediately.”Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsAt the same time, the server maker distanced itself from the actions of the trio. The smuggling caper was described as “a contravention of the Company's policies,” and Super Micro “maintains a robust compliance program and is committed to full adherence to all applicable U.S. export and re-export control laws and regulations.”Nvidia sent a similar themed statement to Tom’s Hardware. “Strict compliance is a top priority for Nvidia. We continue to work closely with our customers and the government on compliance programs as export regulations have expanded,” wrote the AI and graphics chip maker. “Unlawful diversion of controlled U.S. computers to China is a losing proposition across the board—Nvidia does not provide any service or support for such systems, and the enforcement mechanisms are rigorous and effective.”Liaw, Chang, and Sun face some very serious federal felony charges. It is early days in the investigation, but it is possible that these people, in trying to enrich themselves, will end up serving decades (up to 30 years) behind bars, and/or fines, asset forfeiture, and also bans from employment in export-controlled industries. history.author: Mark Tyson
history.titleZh: 超微员工被指控向中国走私价值25亿美元英伟达硬件——案犯使用吹风机在真硬件与数千台假服务器间转移序列号
history.descriptionZh:
近日，美国司法部公布的一份起诉书揭露了一起涉及向中国非法转移尖端人工智能技术的重大案件，三名与服务器制造商超微（Super Micro）有关联的人员被指控参与了一项复杂的走私计划。

**背景与涉案人员**
起诉书指出，美国公民廖逸贤（Yih-Shian "Wally" Liaw，71岁）、台湾地区居民张瑞苍（Ruei-Tsang "Steven" Chang，53岁）与孙廷玮（Ting-Wei "Willy" Sun，44岁）涉嫌合谋，将搭载先进英伟达芯片的服务器非法运往中国。廖逸贤是超微公司的联合创始人，持有价值近5亿美元的公司股份；张瑞苍则是超微在台湾地区的销售经理；孙廷玮则是一名曾与两人合作的第三方经纪人和协调人。目前廖逸贤和孙廷玮已被逮捕，张瑞苍在逃。受此消息影响，超微股价一度下跌12%。

**核心技术原理与走私手法**
该计划的核心是利用美国出口管制法规的漏洞，将本不应售予中国客户的高性能计算设备（即搭载高端英伟达AI GPU的服务器）非法转运。其技术原理并非芯片本身的创新，而是构建了一套精密的物流与文件欺诈体系：
1.  **设立空壳公司**：在东南亚利用一家中间公司作为幌子，伪造最终用户信息。
2.  **伪造文书与“假服务器”**：伪造大量出口文件，并制造了数千台“假服务器”（仅有机箱外壳的空壳设备），用以应付监管检查，营造设备将运往非中国客户的假象。
3.  **“调包”操作**：起诉书和曝光的监控画面显示，涉案人员甚至在仓库中使用吹风机等工具，将真服务器上的序列号标签转移到假服务器外壳上。随后，将拆出的、装载有实际GPU芯片的真服务器部件秘密运往中国。
自2024年以来，该走私网络据信已实现了约25亿美元的销售额。分析人士指出，尽管操作手法周密，但其维持大量假服务器库存、使用吹风机转移标签等行为也显得颇为“荒谬”和大胆。

**性能参数与市场影响**
走私所涉及的是受美国出口管制的高端英伟达AI加速器（如H100、A100等系列）。这些芯片是训练大型语言模型（LLM）和进行高性能计算（HPC）的关键硬件，算力远超对华出口的合规替代品。该案件凸显了国际市场，特别是中国AI行业，对顶级算力的强劲需求与美国出口管制之间的激烈矛盾。值得注意的是，随着美国出口管制政策的动态调整，部分被走私的服务器型号现在可能已能通过申请许可证的方式合法出口。

**公司回应与技术合规影响**
超微公司和英伟达均未在本次起诉中被列为被告。超微发布声明，确认两名涉案员工已被停职，与第三方承包商的关系已被立即终止。公司强调此次事件“违反了公司的政策”，并表示自身拥有健全的合规计划，致力于完全遵守所有美国出口法规。
英伟达也向媒体表示，严格合规是其首要任务，公司持续与客户和政府合作推进合规项目，并指出非法向中国转移受控计算机系统是“全盘皆输的命题”，英伟达不会为此类系统提供任何服务或支持，且执法机制是严格有效的。

**结论与潜在后果**
此案件远非偶然的投机行为，而是一项高度协调、长期运作的走私计划。它严重挑战了美国对华技术出口管制的执行效力，也反映出在全球AI竞赛背景下，尖端算力已成为高度敏感的战略物资。三名被告面临多项联邦重罪指控，若罪名成立，可能面临最高30年的监禁、巨额罚款、资产没收以及被禁止从事受出口管制行业工作等严厉惩罚。这一事件预计将促使监管机构和企业进一步加强供应链审查与合规措施。

history.title: Arctic's $1,400 AMD Strix Point fanless mini-PC hides under your desk — Senza AI 370 features Ryzen AI 9 HX 370 CPU, 32GB RAM, and 1TB SSD
history.description: If you've been looking for a mini PC that goes beyond just decluttering your desk — one that basically doesn't even remind you of its existence, then Arctic has got just the thing for you. The new Senza AI 370 features a powerful AMD chip with a decent iGPU, 32 GB of fast RAM, a 1 TB SSD, and plenty of ports. It costs almost $1,400, though, but at least it's fanless.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 6 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter Arctic has just released a new mini-PC aimed at enthusiasts looking for a clean, minimalist, and silent setup, without compromising on performance. The "Senza AI 370" is the latest in the company's existing Senza lineup that has shipped with the Ryzen 5000 series so far, but it's now been upgraded to AMD's Strix Point. Specifically, it features the Ryzen AI 9 HX 370 flagship APU and a neat trick up its sleeve.Or should we say "below its sleeve"? Because this mini-PC mounts under your desk, completely out of sight, cable management isn't an issue either. Arctic calls it an "Under Desk PC," and it's designed to clear your workspace. You could put it on top of your desk, but that would defeat its entire purpose. You really want to make sure you get the most out of it because it costs 1200 Euros, or almost $1,400.Image 1 of 3(Image credit: Arctic)(Image credit: Arctic)(Image credit: Arctic)For that money, you're getting a top-end chip, the Ryzen AI 370, which has 12 cores and 24 threads that can boost up to 5.1 GHz. It launched almost two years ago, but it was the Red Team's premier laptop offering until Gorgon Point did so a few months ago. It's based on AMD's Zen 5 architecture, featuring 4 standard Zen 5 cores and 8 Zen 5c efficiency cores — both are split off into their own CCX.Article continues below You may like Akasa launches its latest-generation fanless Mini-ITX case Minisforum MS-02 Ultra mini workstation hands-on GMKtec Evo X2 AI Mini PC review: Compact Strix Halo Powerhouse Testing has shown that this hybrid core config doesn't suit either performance or efficiency, since any tasks requiring more cores spill over to the Zen 5c CCX, and there's a large latency penalty for cross-CCX communication. But that's mostly a concern for mobile devices (since this is a mobile chip), a wall-powered mini-PC circumvents these issues and gives you solid performance regardless.(Image credit: Arctic)The Senza AI 370 looks like an AIO radiator for the most part, down to the two tubes poking out of it that terminate in a front panel hub. This detachable box mounts near the edge of your desk, where you can easily interact with it, as it includes a power button, a couple of USB ports, and a headphone jack. The actual PC is meant to go farther back, toward the rear of your table, where it almost disappears.The port selection on the PC itself is very strong: you get 2.5 Gbps Ethernet, USB4, HDMI 2.1, and more, plus Wi-Fi 7 for wireless connectivity. Owing to its design, the entire body is essentially a giant heatsink, with the actual PCB only in the middle, occupying about 30% of the chassis. Eight heatpipes run through this heatsink, cooling the APU without any fans, which allows it to be completely silent. (Image credit: Arctic)Despite nonexistent noise levels, Arctic is claiming great thermals — 50 degrees Celsius under a gaming load, while a "competitor mini PC" reached 67 degrees Celsius. The company also says the Senza reaches 98 FPS in Counter-Strike 2 (at 1080p High), while a similarly equipped mini PC only reaches 86 FPS. This is possible due to the higher-spec RAM configuration on the Senza.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsIt features 32 GB of LPDDR5X-8000 memory, while many other devices use standard DDR5 at slower speeds. The Ryzen AI 370 also has a Radeon 890M iGPU, which is fairly performant for integrated graphics. It's no Strix Halo, but it will get the job done in lightweight games, and can produce decent results in AAA titles with upscaling. It's got 16 RDNA 3.5 Compute Units and can boost up to 2.9 GHz. (Image credit: Arctic)To round out the device, other specs include a 1 TB PCIe 4.0 SSD, support for up to 8K external monitors, pre-installed Windows 11, and, of course, Microsoft Copilot+ branding for AI workloads. You can buy the Senza AI 370 right away from Arctic's website, and while it does cost 1,200 Euros, we noticed that a 170 Euros payback is automatically applied, bringing the price down to 1,029 Euros ($1,192). That's still quite a lot for this performance tier. Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
history.author: Hassam Nasir
history.titleZh: 雅浚1400美元无风扇迷你主机藏身桌底——Senza AI 370搭载锐龙AI 9 HX 370处理器，配备32GB内存与1TB固态硬盘
history.descriptionZh:
知名散热与PC配件制造商Arctic近日推出了一款面向发烧友的全新迷你PC——Senza AI 370。该产品旨在为用户提供一个简洁、极简且完全静音的高性能计算方案，其核心设计理念是“隐形”，可悬挂于桌面下方，彻底摆脱线缆杂乱并释放桌面空间。作为Senza系列的最新成员，它从原有的Ryzen 5000系列平台升级至AMD最新的Strix Point移动平台，搭载了旗舰级APU Ryzen AI 9 HX 370。

**核心硬件与架构创新**
Senza AI 370的核心是Ryzen AI 9 HX 370 APU。这款芯片采用AMD Zen 5架构，拥有12个核心和24个线程，最高加速频率可达5.1 GHz。其核心配置采用混合设计，包含4个标准性能的Zen 5核心和8个专注于能效的Zen 5c核心，且这两组核心分别位于独立的CCX（核心复合体）中。在移动设备上，这种设计可能因跨CCX通信延迟而影响部分多核任务效率，但作为固定接电的迷你PC，电源不受限，可以充分发挥其持续高性能输出的潜力。

图形部分则由Radeon 890M集成显卡负责，其基于RDNA 3.5架构，拥有16个计算单元，加速频率高达2.9 GHz，足以应对轻量级游戏，并在借助超分辨率技术的3A大作中提供尚可的体验。

**独特的“隐形”设计与静音散热**
该产品的最大亮点是其“桌面下PC”的设计。主机本体可安装在桌面后方或下方，几乎完全隐藏。唯一可见的部分是一个可拆卸的前置面板集线器，通过两根导管与主机连接，其上集成了电源键、USB接口和耳机插孔，方便用户日常操作。这种设计将线缆管理和设备藏匿完美结合。

为了实现绝对的静音，Arctic采用了彻底的无风扇散热方案。整个机身实质上是一个巨大的散热器，内部PCB板仅占机箱约30%的空间。八根热管贯穿于这个大型散热体中，将APU产生的热量高效导出并依靠整个金属机壳被动散热。据Arctic称，在游戏负载下，其温度可控制在50摄氏度，而对比的竞品迷你PC则达到了67摄氏度。

**高性能配置与实测表现**
除了强大的APU，该机还配备了高速的32GB LPDDR5X-8000内存。相比许多使用低速标准DDR5内存的设备，这种高带宽内存显著提升了集成显卡的性能和系统响应速度。Arctic提供的测试数据显示，在《反恐精英2》1080p高画质设置下，Senza AI 370能达到98 FPS，而配置相似的竞品仅为86 FPS。

其他规格包括1TB PCIe 4.0 SSD、2.5Gbps有线网口、USB4、HDMI 2.1接口以及Wi-Fi 7无线连接，支持最高8K的外接显示器。设备预装Windows 11，并带有微软Copilot+标识，表明其具备较强的本地AI运算能力。

**市场定位与价格**
Senza AI 370现已通过Arctic官网销售，官方标价为1200欧元（约合1400美元），但结账时会自动减免170欧元，实际入手价格为1029欧元（约1192美元）。尽管价格处于高端区间，但考虑到其顶级的移动APU、独特的隐形设计、完全的静音体验以及出色的散热性能，它为追求极致简洁桌面美学和安静环境的高端用户与发烧友提供了一个颇具吸引力的集成化解决方案。
